narrative: A traveler reaches a long causeway just as dense fog begins to cover the route. Crossing now may save time, but waiting for clearer conditions would reduce the chance of taking a wrong turn or missing a hazard.
high_response: I will wait until the fog clears enough to see the route properly. Saving time is useful, but avoiding an uncertain hazard matters more when the downside could affect the whole journey.
low_response: I will cross now while moving carefully. The route is probably manageable, and accepting some uncertainty is worth it when waiting could cost the opportunity to arrive on time.
